message:
fix(fnp-python): make assert_array_matches_numpy scalar-tolerant

Some numpy entry-points return a plain Python scalar (int / float) rather
than a numpy array when the reduction collapses all dimensions (e.g.
`np.ma.count(ma_1d_array)` with default axis=None returns `int`, not a
0-d array). The assertion helper was unconditionally reading `.dtype` and
`.shape`, which raises AttributeError on a Python scalar.

Added a scalar-detection short-circuit: if `actual.getattr("dtype")` is
Err, require the same of `expected` (symmetry check — if one side is
scalar the other must be too, else we've hit a genuine array-ness
disagreement) and fall back to `repr_string` equality. The existing
array-path (dtype + shape + tolist-repr) still runs for all array-shaped
results.

Test resolved:
  tests::ma_count_matches_numpy_across_axis_and_keepdims (AttributeError:
  'int' object has no attribute 'dtype' → now passes)

Verified via `cargo test -p fnp-python --lib ma_count_matches_numpy` on
CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_franken_numpy_cc — 1 passed, 0 failed.

Pre-existing failure tracked in UPGRADE_LOG.md (2026-04-22 session notes)
is now resolved.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
